title: Return of the Jedi (arcade game)

The '''''Return of the Jedi''''' debuted in video arcades in [[1984]]. Levels in the game are set during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Digitized voices of various actors' lines from the film play at certain moments in the game. During the recording of the player's records on the high score screen, the [[Ewok Celebration]] song will play in the background.

The game was later ported to home platforms including [[Amstrad CPC]], [[Atari ST]], [[BBC Micro]], [[Wikipedia:Commodore 64|Commodore 64]], and [[ZX Spectrum]]. It also appeared in the Arcade mode of ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'', like the other two Arcade games ''[[Star Wars: The Arcade Game]]'' and [[The Empire Strikes Back (arcade game)|''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. Unlike those two, however, it can't be unlocked by regular means, and requires a two-part passcode to unlock. The passcode is "!?ATH!RD GAME?YES".

{{Game-stub}}

==Gameplay==
There are eight levels and each level is divided into three phases: in the first phase you control [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Princess Leia]] on a [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] and you are pursued by Imperial speeders trying to take you down. In the second phase two sequences are alternated: in one the player controls an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] as [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] and has to avoid tree trunks coming down, while in the other the player controls the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'' as [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], escorted by two [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] facing groups of three [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]] each, flying close to [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyers]]. The last phase enters [[Death Star II/Legends|the second Death Star]] with the ''Falcon'' alone and the player has to blow up the reactor while avoiding pursuing TIE interceptors.
